audit_result.proof_trace: BlobSchedule deserialization and construction lack validation that max_blobs_per_block <= MAX_BLOB_COMMITMENTS_PER_BLOCK (4096). Config entries can specify arbitrary values (e.g., 5000) which are accepted without bounds checking, creating type-safety violations and potential consensus splits.
audit_result.attack_scenario: Attacker provides ChainSpec config with BLOB_SCHEDULE entry containing max_blobs_per_block=5000. Nodes load config successfully, then accept blocks with 4500 blob commitments (passing runtime check at block_verification.rs:885) but violating SSZ VariableList constraints, causing divergent validation behavior.

PROP-56ad1eb2-inv-014.text: All BLOB_SCHEDULE max_blobs_per_block values must be <= MAX_BLOB_COMMITMENTS_PER_BLOCK.
PROP-56ad1eb2-inv-014.assertion: forall entry in BLOB_SCHEDULE: entry.max_blobs_per_block <= MAX_BLOB_COMMITMENTS_PER_BLOCK
